steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: One gallon of sealant covers 190 square feet. So to cover $A$ square feet once, you need $\frac{A}{190}$ gallons.

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: The deck has area $w$ square feet and must be sealed twice, so the total area to cover is $2w$ square feet.

choices.explanation: • This formula covers the deck only once, not twice. The problem requires two coats, so the total area is $2w$, not $w$. This is incorrect.
• This multiplies $w$ by 190 instead of dividing. Larger deck area means more gallons needed, but the rate is gallons per square foot = $\frac{1}{190}$, not 190. This is incorrect.
• This is $190 \times 2 \times w$, which multiplies by the coverage rate instead of dividing by it. This is not correct.
• Total area for two coats = $2w$. Gallons = $\frac{2w}{190} = \frac{w}{95}$. That is the correct answer.
